title: Original Eva DVDs Worth Hanging on to
display_title: Original Eva DVDs Worth Hanging on to
wikitext:
[]: Original Eva DVD's Worth Hanging on to.             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Mon Jan 22, 2007 2:27 am Okay, I know just about everyone here has the new ultra spiffy platinum version of NGE, but in my own opinion, I like the original DVD's without all the bells and whistles. http://i10.photobucket.com/albums/a149/KhakiBlueThunderSocks/83b42060.jpg Granted I do have two platinum DVD's I found for a great price at Sam Goode and EBGames (Sadly, the thin-pack DVD I bought was volume 03 with no special goodies) and the special "Directors Cut: Resurection" DVD I found for about $5, but other than that, my collection is all the original DVD's. Am I the only one who still has the originals?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 22, 2007 2:54 am The only reason I would suggest having them around is for collective purposes. Kind of like the first editions of the Eva manga by Viz. But SRYSLY, Platinum is so much better. Not having frame jitter alone makes it worth the 50 bucks at Wal-Mart.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Mon Jan 22, 2007 3:00 am I really don't notice things like frame jitter or stuff like that. Granted, the Platinum and DA DVD's I do have are cleaner than the originals, I really focus on what's going on in the anime, not the frames or stuff like that. I'm a simple country boy from Louisiana! You have Eva in YOUR Wal-Mart?!

[]:             Quote Post by Ejinathan » Mon Jan 22, 2007 9:20 am well i dont have it at a wall mart anyway (begium is almost like anti-manga for some few exeptions). I only bought the perfect collection who has to arrive sooner or later and that's enough for me.....(btw got the dvd of death and rebirth + eoe also) Btw, i see u got 2 times end of evangelion rofl. wasn't the dvd of death and rebirth + eoe not good enough ? I just ask this because you also got another cd of eoe..... :p.

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Mon Jan 22, 2007 9:36 am Here in Spain, different versions were released. I still keep the original VHS of the series, and a fansub VHS of The End of Evangelion. Later, I bought the limited DVD boxset containing the whole series (a beautiful book-like package), and the DVD boxset containing the movies. But there have been like 3 or 4 different DVD releases of Evangelion in Span.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Mon Jan 22, 2007 10:10 am The originals are all I have, too. I even have the glitched EoE. :P

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 22, 2007 2:44 pm KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: I really don't notice things like frame jitter or stuff like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 22, 2007 5:08 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: I really don't notice things like frame jitter or stuff like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jan 22, 2007 9:07 pm Reichu wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: I really don't notice things like frame jitter or stuff like that.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jan 22, 2007 9:38 pm I sold my original Eva boxset the second they announced the Platinum editions. That went a long way in funding buying all those singles, believe me. To be honest I kind of like the "jitter" just because it was unique and quirky to Eva alone. But the improved picture quality and 5.1 more than make up for that in the new editions.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Jan 22, 2007 9:47 pm For my first ever viewing of Eva, I jacked some low quality original prints to watch over the internets. When I got those Platinums, the difference was incredible.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Mon Jan 22, 2007 9:55 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: I really don't notice things like frame jitter or stuff like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 22, 2007 11:06 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: To be honest I kind of like the "jitter" just because it was unique and quirky to Eva alone.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Jan 23, 2007 5:13 pm My Grandma's not pretty to look at. She's missing a leg and she's very old, thus making her visually unpleasant. Yet I hang on to her because she means a lot to me. I wuv her. :cry: That's also how I feel about my old anime Dvd's including Eva. If something new and better comes out, I'll usually get it but also hang on to the older stuff as well. Not because I watch it all the time, but because I wuv them. Is that so weird?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 23, 2007 5:52 pm The analogy is WAYYYYYY too loose to work, you know.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Jan 23, 2007 6:58 pm Comparing my one legged grandmother to pieces of plastic that display information when placed in an optical drive too much of a stretch for ya? Fehh, Weirdo. But seriously, dosen't anyone have an old video game/DVD that they know there never going to play again, but hold on to for sentimental reasons? I know I do (Every Mario Platform game ever made comes to mind)

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Jan 24, 2007 4:39 pm I never throw any of my old stuff away. I still have my Perfect collection and Director Cuts along with the platinum collection. And I will keep those even after I get the collectors edition if it ever comes out. Just because you have the latest and greatest of something doesn't mean you should throw the old version out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 24, 2007 10:04 pm Hunter21 wrote: I never throw any of my old stuff away. I still have my Perfect collection and Director Cuts along with the platinum collection. And I will keep those even after I get the collectors edition if it ever comes out. Just because you have the latest and greatest of something doesn't mean you should throw the old version out.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 25, 2007 3:08 pm Platinum is the first time around I've obtained the series while I'm holding off for the US release of Renewal The Feature Films. (Sometime TODAY, Mangle! Jeez, can't ADV just get the rights to prevent Mangle from fucking it up yet again?) But yeah, compared to what you see online of the series via fansubs, Platinum's quality IS incredible! :shock:
